control_design.primary_signal: hit-set membership across old-query and new-query search results
control_design.notes: Single memory in collection; nearest-neighbor search top_k=5 for old and new queries could not distinguish 'stored vector retained' from 'new vector correctly written but still nearest-of-one'. The under-controlled design is preserved here so the retraction is auditable.

limitations: no direct inspection of stored embedding
hit-set membership treated as evidence of stored-vector identity
conclusion_narrative: V08 concluded that Mem0 does not refresh the derived vector on update(text=...). The classification 'gap' and claim_status 'confirmed' record what V08 asserted at the moment the record was written. Effective disposition is determined by the chain — record v08.1.scenario7.controlled_experiment later retracts this claim. This record is preserved unchanged as required by the R6 append-only protocol.

revises.reason: The V08 design used a single memory in the collection, so a nearest-neighbor search of top_k=5 returning that memory for both old and new queries could not distinguish 'stored vector retained' from 'new vector correctly written but still nearest-of-one'. Direct inspection of the stored embedding was not attempted. Under a properly controlled design (five distinctive control memories plus direct fetch of stored embedding bytes), Mem0 does refresh the derived vector.

negative_controls: five unrelated distinctive memories populated in the same collection so nearest-neighbor rank is meaningful
confounders_considered: hybrid lexical retrieval scoring masking a retained vector
confounders_ruled_out: ranking-only inference (bypassed by direct backend inspection of stored embedding)
control_design.notes: Read-only inspection of the configured Chroma backend via mem.vector_store.collection.get(ids=..., include=['embeddings']) was used as the primary signal. Retrieval rank/score was corroborating only, not primary.

limitations: embedding-model HuggingFace snapshot revision is not pinned; a future revision could shift retrieval rank/score without invalidating the qualitative conclusion
evidence_source 'backend_inspection' is legitimate but is not a Mem0 public-API probe
conclusion_narrative: Under a corrected experimental design (five distinctive control memories, maximally unrelated old/new phrases, direct fetch of stored embedding from the Chroma collection) Mem0 refreshes the derived vector on update(text=...). Stored embedding bytes changed with L2 delta ~ 1.374 across unit-norm vectors; retrieval-rank signal corroborates. This record retracts v08.scenario7.probe.
